title: Tender Irish Daikon Chowder
description: A winey, creamy Irish chowder with sashimi-sliced daikon, perfect for a solo low‑glycemic dinner.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a medium pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add sliced daikon and onion, sauté for 3 minutes until slightly translucent.
Stir in minced garlic and sliced mushrooms, cooking another 2 minutes.
Pour in dry white wine, letting it reduce by half over 4 minutes.
Add vegetable broth and lemon juice, bringing the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 10 minutes until the daikon is tender.
Stir in light coconut milk and chopped thyme, reheating until hot but not boiling.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste, adjusting seasoning as needed.
Remove from heat, pour the chowder into a bowl, and serve hot with a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil.
Garnish with a few fresh thyme sprigs or a light squeeze of lemon if desired.
